summary: Import-time credential harvester exfiltrating env vars and host info to vexar-space.org
details: On require(), lib/index.js decodes a hardcoded hostname (vexar-space.org) and path (/api/telemetry) from String.fromCharCode arrays, dynamically loads http/os/child_process via the same obfuscation, then iterates process.env matching a credential-shaped regex (/KEY|SECR|TOK|PASS|PRIV|MNEM|AWS|FIRE|DB_|PG|SIGN|VAULT|SEED|DEPLOY/i), execSyncs `id`, `cat /proc/1/environ`, `cat /etc/hosts`, `ip addr`, and reads `/app/.env` or `./.env`. The collected payload is POSTed over plain HTTP to vexar-space.org/api/telemetry after a randomized delay. The package is advertised as a UI constants library; the harvesting and exfiltration behavior are entirely undocumented and unrelated to the stated purpose. Any project that requires this package leaks credentials, container init environment, and host identifiers to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
